summary: Russian tactical aviation has launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, while a Tu-22M3 bomber was relocated from Olenya to Dygilevo. Reactive and cruise UAV activity is reported across Chernihiv, Zhytomyr, and Poltava oblasts, and Russian forces claim to have liberated the village of Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk. Ukrainian forces continue to hold the front at Mala Tokmachka for over 1500 days despite ongoing attacks.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, indicating intensified air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
A Tu-22M3 bomber was transferred from the Olenya airfield to the Dygilevo airfield, suggesting repositioning of strategic assets (Source: @eRadarrua/93716).
Reactive UAVs traveling over 500 km/h were observed in Chernihiv and other northern regions, highlighting increased high‑speed drone threats (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).
Russian forces announced the liberation of Dobropasovo village near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Two Russian air‑dropped bombs struck Kramatorsk city centre, injuring at least five civilians and damaging multi‑storey buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
Ukrainian defenders of Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia region have maintained the line for over 1500 days, setting a national record (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian Foreign Ministry warned foreigners to leave Kyiv and announced strikes against Ukrainian defense industry facilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk, while a Tu-22M3 bomber was relocated to Dygilevo. High‑speed reactive UAVs are active across northern oblasts, and Russian forces claim to have liberated Dobropasovo. Ukrainian forces continue a prolonged defence at Mala Tokmachka despite sustained attacks.

points: Ukrainian defenders of the village Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia region have held the front line for more than 1500 days, earning a place in the national record book (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).

Two Russian air‑dropped bombs struck the centre of Kramatorsk, causing at least five civilian injuries and damage to multi‑storey buildings, with rescue operations ongoing (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
Ukrainian officials reported that reactive UAVs traveling over 500 km/h were observed in Chernihiv and surrounding areas, indicating a heightened drone threat (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).

Ukrainian Defense Ministry advisors noted that Russian forces struck the Ukrainian Ground Forces command building and the Lukyanovka/Artem missile plant, though the command building had not been used since the war began (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, as reported by multiple channel posts (Source: @kpszsu/62777, @kpszsu/62797, @kpszsu/62782).
A Tu-22M3 strategic bomber was relocated from the Olenya airfield to the Dygilevo airfield, moving closer to Ukraine's border (Source: @eRadarrua/93716).

The Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs warned foreign nationals to leave Kyiv and announced consistent strikes against Ukrainian defense industry facilities and command posts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
Russian state media reported the liberation of the village Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Guided bomb attacks were reported on Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, indicating intensified Russian air operations on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62777, @kpszsu/62797).
Ukrainian ground forces reported damage to the Lukyanovka/Artem missile plant in the east, hit by Russian strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Reactive UAV activity was noted near Snovsk and other eastern locations, with some drones heading south‑east (Source: @eRadarrua/93693).
Russian forces claim to have liberated Dobropasovo village in Dnipropetrovsk, a strategic point on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
High‑speed reactive UAVs (>500 km/h) were observed over Chernihiv, indicating increased aerial threats in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).
Multiple UAV sightings reported in Chernihiv oblast, including routes toward Novhorod‑Siverskyi, Ripky, and Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/62808, @eRadarrua/93714).
A reactive UAV was reported near Losynivka in Chernihiv, adding to the northern drone activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93718).
Guided bomb launches by Russian tactical aviation were also reported targeting the northern Zaporizhzhia area (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Ukrainian forces continue to defend Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia region, holding the line for over 1500 days (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian guided bomb attacks were reported on Zaporizhzhia oblast, affecting southern front positions (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Ukrainian officials noted that Russian strikes targeted decision‑making centers and command posts across Ukraine, including southern regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
No specific casualty figures were provided for southern front engagements in the source messages.
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on multiple fronts, including Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/62777, @kpszsu/62797, @kpszsu/62782).
Two air‑dropped bombs struck Kramatorsk city centre, causing civilian injuries and property damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
High‑speed reactive UAVs (>500 km/h) were observed over Chernihiv and other northern oblasts, indicating a surge in drone activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).
A Tu-22M3 bomber was relocated from Olenya to Dygilevo, suggesting repositioning of strategic air assets (Source: @eRadarrua/93716).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia, Donetsk, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, intensifying air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
A Tu-22M3 strategic bomber was moved from Olenya to Dygilevo, positioning it closer to the Ukrainian border (Source: @eRadarrua/93716).
High‑speed reactive UAVs traveling over 500 km/h were observed in Chernihiv and surrounding northern areas, raising drone threat levels (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).
Russian forces announced the liberation of Dobropasovo village near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Two Russian air‑dropped bombs hit Kramatorsk city centre, injuring at least five civilians and damaging buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
Ukrainian defenders have held the front at Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia for over 1500 days, setting a national defence record (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
The Russian Foreign Ministry warned foreigners to leave Kyiv and announced strikes against Ukrainian defense industry facilities and command posts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30420).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air operations are escalating, with guided bomb deployments across multiple fronts and the repositioning of a Tu-22M3 strategic bomber, indicating a focus on deep‑strike capabilities. High‑speed reactive UAVs are proliferating in northern oblasts, posing a growing threat to ground forces and infrastructure. Ukrainian defensive resilience remains notable, especially at Mala Tokmachka, but sustained aerial pressure could strain command and logistics.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defence and ground positions in response to increased bombings and UAV activity, particularly in the north and east. Civilian emergency services are active in Kramatorsk, handling casualties and damage. International observers note continued Russian rhetoric targeting Kyiv's leadership and industrial sites, potentially influencing diplomatic engagements.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Tu-22M3 movements suggests possible future long‑range strike missions; satellite and SIGINT assets should prioritize tracking flight paths from Dygilevo. UAV sightings across Chernihiv, Zhytomyr, and Poltava oblasts require enhanced electronic warfare and counter‑drone measures. The claim of Dobropasovo's liberation should be corroborated with on‑ground reconnaissance to assess actual control.
